metadata.title: States PCS: West Bengal GK Questions for WBPSC Examinations

questions.question: Which is the greatest festival of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Durga Puja is the most important and the greatest festival of West Bengal. The festival is celebrated for five days. During these five days, different rituals and pujas are observed. These five days are known as Shashthi, Saptami, Ashtami, Navami and Dashami.

questions.question: What is the main performer of Charak Puja called?
questions.explanation: The festival of Charak Puja is devoted to the deities Shiva and Shakti. This festival can be seen in the Kolkata, Siliguri, Jalpaiguri part of West Bengal which is an urban area but the festival is very famous in these places. Charak puja is performed by usually ten to twelve members, including both men and women. The bearers of the ritual are called Charkia and the main performer Deoboinshi.

questions.question: Which was the first club to win Durand Cup in 1940?
questions.explanation: Mohammedan SC Club was found in 1891 in Kolkata. It is one of the oldest and most popular football clubs in India. Players of this club played against Britishers and earned huge support from all over India. It was the first Indian club to win the Durand Cup in 1940 and the first Indian club to win on foreign land. This club was also the first club to win Aga Khan Gold Cup in 1950.

questions.question: In which year Calcutta Cricket and Football Club was founded?
questions.explanation: Calcutta Cricket and Football Club was founded in 1792 and is one of the oldest sports clubs in the world. This present day Club has merged with the well known clubs such as Calcutta Cricket Club of 1792 vintage, Ballygunge Cricket Club (1864-1950) and Calcutta Football Club (1872-1877).

questions.question: Out of total 91.3 million people of West Bengal, how many (approx.) people follow Buddhism?
questions.explanation: Buddhism is the fourth largest of West Bengal with about 0.31% people responded that they were Buddhists during the 2011 Census of India. Out of total 91.3 million people in the state, the Buddhist population is approximately 2.82 lakhs. Also out of 23 districts in West Bengal, Buddhists have a higher concentration in Darjeeling.

questions.question: Sub-alpine Forests are found in–
questions.explanation: Sub-alpine Forests is found in the very high reaches of North Bengal hills with an altitude between 3000m-3700m in the Northern part of Darjeeling district. The main sites of these forests are Sandakphu, Sabarkum, Phalut, etc.

questions.question: How many Municipal Corporations in total are there in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: In West Bengal, there are 7 Municipal Corporations. Kolkata, the state capital and a metropolitan city, is managed by Kolkata Municipal Corporation (KMC). Bidhannagar is managed by Bidhannagar Municipality. Chandannagar that was a French colony during British rule has now a Municipal Corporation. Siliguri which is an important railway junction and the gateway to North-East is managed by Siliguri Municipal Corporation.

questions.question: Who are the head of Municipal Corporation?
questions.explanation: The State Government constitutes municipalities after taking into consideration the public opinion of the areas concerned. Municipal areas are divided into several wards, mainly depending on the population size of the municipality. Each ward is represented by a member being elected directly by the people of that ward. Mayors/ Chairpersons of the municipal bodies are directly elected by the councillors.

questions.question: In which year Jhulan Goswami was awarded the Sportsperson of the Year honor at the Calcutta Sports Journalists’ Club?
questions.explanation: On 23rd April 2019, Jhulan Goswami, former captain of India National Women’s Cricket team, awarded the Sportsperson of the Year honor at the Calcutta Sports Journalists’ Club (CSJC) annual awards function 2019 held at Kolkata.

questions.question: Which famous former football captain passed away in May 2020?
questions.explanation: Former India football captain Subimal Goswami, commonly known by his nickname Chuni Goswami, passed away at the age of 82 on May in Kolkata, West Bengal. Under the captaincy of Goswami, the Indian Football team won gold at the 1962 Asian Games. He won the Arjuna Award in 1963 and was awarded the prestigious Padma Shri Award in 1983.
